page_title: Francophone Community Immigration Pilot: Get your language assessed - Canada.ca

h2: How to find your language level based on your test results
h3: What to include in your application

sections.heading: Language tests we accept
items: You can take any of these approved language tests:

sections.heading: French
bullets: TEF Canada: Test d’évaluation de français
TCF Canada : Test de connaissance du français

sections.heading: Minimum scores needed
items: You need a minimum score of NCLC 5 in all 4 abilities to apply for the Francophone Community Immigration Pilot (FCIP).

sections.heading: How to find your language level based on your test results
items: Use your test results to find your NCLC level in the tables below:

sections.heading: What to include in your application
items: If you meet the minimum score in all 4 abilities, include a copy of the test results with your application.
These results must be less than 2 years old when you apply. Keep a copy of them for your records and future use.
